summary: DHH reflects on the aftermath of 37signals' 2021 decision to ban political discussions at work, now vindicated by Google adopting a similar policy. He describes the internet mob backlash as ultimately a gift that revealed true friends and allies. He argues that the social justice movement of the late 2010s swept up many well-meaning people into divisive ideology, and that the path forward is forgiveness rather than retaliation. He calls for a broad societal rejection of the ideas themselves while extending grace to individuals who were temporarily caught up in them.
key_points: Google's adoption of a no-politics-at-work policy echoes what 37signals and Coinbase did years earlier, suggesting vindication
Being the target of internet mob outrage, while awful in the moment, served as a clarifying gift that revealed true allies
The social justice movement of the late 2010s through 2022 swept otherwise kind people into divisive oppressor/oppressed frameworks
Pursuing vendettas against individuals who were momentarily swept up in bad ideas will slow the broader rejection of those ideas
The winning strategy against ideological rigidity is to be broader, more forgiving, and less punitive — the opposite of eating your own
money_quotes: In the moment, it was awful, but in retrospect, it was a gift.
We walk through life much of the time without really knowing who'll be there when the going gets tough -- and our guesses are often wrong.
It brought unexpected friends and allies out in the light, and it revealed which friends and acquaintances would rather crawl back in the woodwork than stand by my side.
It swept up so many otherwise kind and caring people into an ideology predicated on dividing us all into oppressors/oppressed, privileged/not, and other false dichotomies and identities.
The worst thing we can do to slow down the rejection of these bad ideas is by forever tarring people who were momentarily taken in by them.
It kept eating its own for ever-smaller transgressions against an ever-shifting doctrine.
To beat that nonsense back, the side of sanity has to do the opposite. Be broader, more forgiving, and less high strung.
key_insight: The most effective way to defeat a divisive ideology is not retaliation against its followers but broad forgiveness that makes it easy for people to walk away from bad ideas.
